campus: Torino (Main Campus), Politecnico di Torino. Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering (DIMEAS).
double_degree_or_erasmus_mundus: Not an Erasmus Mundus Joint Master. The programme offers a double-degree option via Erasmus+ with the University of Windsor (Canada). The standard single-degree route is admission-by-evaluation; the double-degree track may have its own selection and is not required for normal admission. [uncertain]
admission_test_type: No entrance test. Admission is by evaluation of qualification/curriculum (curricular requirements + suitable academic performance) carried out by professors/Academic Advisor appointed by the Coordinator of the Collegio. For non-Italian-qualification applicants the evaluation is run on the Apply@polito platform.
applicant_route: Applicants with an Italian qualification (Politecnico di Torino graduates)
Applicants with an Italian qualification (graduates of other Italian universities)
Non-EU applicants residing in Italy (with valid residence permit)
Non-EU applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa (the route for Turkish applicants with a non-Italian qualification)
admission_call_round: 1st call (A.Y. 2026/27, non-Italian qualification): opening 19 December 2025; closing 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. (Italian time)
2nd call (A.Y. 2026/27, non-Italian qualification): opening 18 March 2026; closing 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. (Italian time)

Note: deadlines extended by 15 days by decree DR 96/2026. No programme-specific special call applies to Automotive Engineering — it follows the standard Engineering-area evaluation calls (admission is uncapped/evaluation-based).
application_platform: Apply@polito (didattica.polito.it Teaching Portal, 'applicants with a non-Italian qualification' section) for the online application and evaluation. Non-EU applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa must additionally submit a pre-enrolment application via the UNIVERSITALY portal to obtain a type-D study visa.

academic_requirements.qualification: A three-year Bachelor's degree / university diploma, or another educational qualification obtained abroad and recognized as suitable for admission.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirement_basic_sciences: Minimum 40 credits (CFU/ECTS) in the Scientific Disciplinary Fields (SSD): CHIM/07, FIS/01, FIS/03, ING-INF/05, MAT/02, MAT/03, MAT/05.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirement_specific_subjects: Minimum 60 credits (CFU/ECTS) in the SSD of specific subjects: ING-IND/08, ING-IND/10, ING-IND/12, ING-IND/13, ING-IND/14, ING-IND/15, ING-IND/16, ING-IND/21, ING-IND/22, ING-IND/31, ING-IND/32, ING-IND/35, ING-INF/01. (Credits appearing in both groups are first counted toward the 40-credit group, remainder toward the 60-credit group.)
academic_requirements.shortfall_handling: Applicants lacking fewer than 10 credits can be admitted by the Academic Advisor. Lacking more than 10 credits is subject to approval by the Coordinator/Vice-coordinator. Those not meeting curricular requirements must make up missing credits before enrolment via single courses (up to 60 credits) or Bachelor-level credit transfer (more than 60 credits).
academic_requirements.academic_performance: Applicants must have suitable academic performance plus an English certificate at CEFR B2 or above.
academic_requirements.non_italian_qualification_specific: Applicants with a non-Italian qualification must hold a degree from an accredited/recognized foreign university earned after at least 15 years of total education (primary + secondary + university), with at least 3 years at university level and at least 180 ECTS credits (or equivalent). Pre-university/foundation years do not count toward the minimum years or credits. Academic performance and consistency with the PoliTo programme are assessed by appointed professors on Apply@polito.

country_specific_eligibility: Turkish applicants hold a non-Italian qualification and are treated as non-EU. They must satisfy the 15-years-of-total-education / 180-ECTS / 3-years-university rule and submit their bachelor's qualification with a Declaration of Value (Dichiarazione di Valore) issued by the competent Italian Embassy/Consulate OR a CIMEA Statement of Comparability (compulsory for enrolment). No Turkey-specific table was published on the official pages; general non-Italian-qualification rules apply. [uncertain]
minimum_background_or_credits: Minimum 40 ECTS in basic-science SSD (CHIM/07, FIS/01, FIS/03, ING-INF/05, MAT/02, MAT/03, MAT/05) AND minimum 60 ECTS in specific automotive/mechanical SSD (ING-IND/08, 10, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 21, 22, 31, 32, 35; ING-INF/01). Grade thresholds (weighted average on /30 after subtracting the worst 28 credits): PoliTo graduates — none if degree earned in <=4 years, >=21/30 if 4-5 years, >=24/30 if >5 years; other Italian-university graduates — >=24/30 regardless of duration.

language_requirements.english: English language certificate at CEFR B2 level or above is mandatory for admission to this fully English-taught programme. Accepted certificates are those in PoliTo's official table (IELTS, TOEFL iBT, TOEIC, Cambridge English, Pearson PTE, etc.).
language_requirements.italian: No Italian certificate is required (the entire programme is taught in English).
language_requirements.exemptions: Holders of a qualification taught fully in English are generally exempt; check the official certificate table/Annex for exemption criteria. [uncertain]
language_requirements.submission: The English certificate must be uploaded in the application during the relevant call (and is verified as part of the evaluation/enrolment process).

english_certificate_codes: For TOEFL/TOEIC, applicants indicate PoliTo institution code C285. For Cambridge, share results online and provide the Candidate ID number.
language_submission_timing: The English (B2+) certificate must be submitted with the online application during the chosen call; it is required for a positive evaluation and for enrolment. Exact per-call language cut-off date for masters not separately published beyond the call closing dates. [uncertain]

application_deadline_eu.1st_call: Opening 19 December 2025; closing 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. (Italian time).
application_deadline_eu.2nd_call: Opening 18 March 2026; closing 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. (Italian time).
application_deadline_eu.note: Same call windows apply to EU applicants, non-EU residing in Italy, and non-EU residing abroad. Deadlines extended by 15 days by decree DR 96/2026. Online enrolment (1st semester) closes 5 October 2026; 2nd-semester enrolment (only EU and non-EU residing in Italy) 13 November 2026 - 24 March 2027.

application_deadline_non_eu.1st_call: Opening 19 December 2025; closing 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. (Italian time). Results around 15 April 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.2nd_call: Opening 18 March 2026; closing 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. (Italian time). Results around 22 June 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.study_visa_preenrolment: UNIVERSITALY pre-enrolment for the study visa: by 30 May 2026 (1st call) / by 15 July 2026 (2nd call).
application_deadline_non_eu.note: Deadlines extended 15 days by decree DR 96/2026. Turkish applicants (non-EU, visa-required) use this route.

universitaly_preenrolment_deadline: Non-EU applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa must submit the visa pre-enrolment application via the UNIVERSITALY portal by 30 May 2026 (1st call) or by 15 July 2026 (2nd call) - separate from, and in addition to, the Apply@polito application. UNIVERSITALY pre-enrolment is mandatory for a type-D study visa and does not replace the PoliTo eligibility procedure.
application_fee: No application/evaluation fee for the master's admission is stated on the official non-Italian-qualification pages (master's admission is by evaluation, not by a paid test). [uncertain]
required_documents: Passport (or ID card for EU nationals)
Residence permit (non-EU citizens residing in Italy)
Bachelor's degree / graduation documentation (degree certificate or final-degree statement)
Syllabus / course descriptions (to assess curricular SSD credits)
English language certificate at CEFR B2 or above (or valid exemption)
Dichiarazione di Valore (Declaration of Value) of the Bachelor's degree OR CIMEA Statement of Comparability (compulsory for enrolment)
For enrolment (non-EU residing abroad): type-D Student Visa and UNIVERSITALY pre-enrolment confirmation
document_translation_authentication: Foreign qualifications require either a Declaration of Value (Dichiarazione di Valore) issued by the competent Italian Embassy/Consulate or a CIMEA Statement of Comparability issued by the Italian ENIC-NARIC centre (compulsory for enrolment). Documents not in Italian/English typically require an official translation. Turkish-language documents must be accompanied by an official English/Italian translation. [uncertain]
entry_exam_or_test: No entry exam or admission test. Admission is by evaluation of the qualification/curriculum: verification of curricular requirements (SSD credit minimums) plus suitable academic performance (grade thresholds and/or consistency assessment). For non-Italian-qualification applicants the evaluation is performed by appointed professors on the Apply@polito platform, with a positive evaluation constituting an offer of admission valid only for the academic year of application.
test_score_or_threshold: No test score. Grade thresholds for academic performance (weighted average /30, after subtracting the worst 28 credits): PoliTo graduates - none if bachelor completed in <=4 years, >=21/30 if 4-5 years, >=24/30 if >5 years; other Italian-university graduates - >=24/30 regardless of duration. Non-Italian-qualification applicants are assessed for academic performance and consistency by appointed professors (no fixed numeric cut-off published). Missing curricular credits trigger make-up requirements (single courses up to 60 CFU; credit transfer above 60 CFU).

source_quotes.quote: Degree Class: LM-33: Mechanical Engineering ... English-taught ... developed into four different pathways.
Admissions: Uncapped number of students with evaluation of admission requirements.

applicants must have earned: minimum 40 credits in the following Scientific Disciplinary Fields ... CHIM/07, FIS/01, FIS/03, ING-INF/05, MAT/02, MAT/03, MAT/05; minimum 60 credits in the following Scientific Disciplinary Fields ... ING-IND/08, ING-IND/10, ING-IND/12, ING-IND/13, ING-IND/14, ING-IND/15, ING-IND/16, ING-IND/21, ING-IND/22, ING-IND/31, ING-IND/32, ING-IND/35, ING-INF/01.
Applicants must have a suitable academic performance and an English language certificate (B2 level or above, as defined by the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages ... CEFR).

Applicants from Politecnico di Torino ... 4 years or less - no exam average grade required; between 4 and 5 years - exam weighted average grade required: >=21/30; more than 5 years - exam weighted average grade required: >= 24/30. ... Applicants who have a Bachelor's degree awarded by another Italian university must have a weighted average grade of all the exams >= 24/30.

To be admitted to Politecnico Master's degree programmes, applicants must have an academic qualification awarded by an accredited/recognized foreign university, earned after completing at least 15 years of total education ... they must have earned at least 180 ECTS credits or equivalent. ... The evaluation is carried out on the Apply@polito platform under the section called 'applicants with a non-Italian qualification.'

By decree DR 96/2026, the deadlines for the admission procedures to the Master's degree programmes for the academic year 2026/2027, intended for students holding a foreign qualification, have been extended by 15 days. 1st Call closing 13 February 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time); 2nd Call closing 20 April 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time).

If you are an international student residing abroad and you wish to apply for a study visa, you must submit your pre-enrolment application through the UNIVERSITALY portal ... Pre-enrolment deadline (Study Visa): 30 May 2026 (1st call) / 15 July 2026 (2nd call).

Required documents: Passport, Residence permit (non-EU in Italy), CV, Bachelor's degree or graduation documentation, Official Transcript of Records, Syllabus/Course descriptions, Language Certificate, and Dichiarazione di valore or Statement of Comparability issued by CIMEA (if available, but compulsory for enrolment). TOEFL/TOEIC: code C285; Cambridge: Candidate ID.
Students may pursue a double degree at the University of Windsor (Canada) through Erasmus+.

uncertainty_notes: BROAD English rule confirmed TRUE: the official programme page lists the degree class as 'LM-33' with teaching language 'English-taught', and the Academic Regulations explicitly state 'All courses are English-taught.' is_fully_english_programme = TRUE and has_complete_english_track = TRUE; english_track_name is null because the whole programme (and all four Year-2 specialist tracks: Autonomous and Connected Vehicles, Sustainable Propulsion Systems, Advanced Product Development, Advanced Manufacturing Engineering) is in English.

official_call_url: no single programme-specific 'Call for Applications' PDF exists for Automotive Engineering because master's admission is uncapped/evaluation-based (not a ranked selection call). The governing official sources are the non-Italian-qualification master's admission page and the Automotive Engineering Academic Regulations PDF. The Academic Regulations PDF retrieved is labelled A.Y. 2025/2026 (latest published); the 2026/27 programme page confirms the same uncapped evaluation structure, but the 2026/27 regulations PDF may differ slightly in wording.

Application deadlines (1st call closing 13 Feb 2026; 2nd call closing 20 Apr 2026; UNIVERSITALY 30 May / 15 Jul 2026; enrolment docs by 5 Oct 2026; type-D visa by 30 Nov 2026) come from the official PoliTo non-Italian-qualification master's page reflecting the DR 96/2026 15-day extension. The separate didattica.polito.it/inte deadline pages still display cached 2023/24 data and were NOT used for dates.
No master's application/evaluation fee is documented on the official non-Italian-qualification pages; flagged uncertain because a per-application fee was not explicitly confirmed as absent.

Country-specific (Turkey) details: no Turkey-specific qualification table was published; the general non-Italian-qualification rules (15 years total education, 180 ECTS, 3 years university, Declaration of Value or CIMEA) were applied. CIMEA Statement of Comparability is compulsory for enrolment.

English certificate exemptions and the exact per-call language certificate cut-off date for masters were not pinned to a single explicit sentence; the certificate table (Annex/CLA) lists accepted certificates and minimum scores but the live CLA certificates URL redirected and did not render the full table. B2+ requirement and code C285 are confirmed.

double_degree_or_erasmus_mundus: the University of Windsor (Canada) double degree via Erasmus+ is mentioned on the programme-details page; its separate selection/fees were not detailed and are flagged uncertain. It is an optional track, not required for standard admission.
tuition_or_fees_link points to the official PoliTo right-to-education/tuition hub; the specific master's fee amounts are on the linked '/tuition-fees' subpage and were not enumerated here.
